revision.comment: See also hatnote to [[Help:References and page numbers#Named references]] from section: Citing different pages in a single source
revision.text: {{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Wikipedia sources]]

{{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Wikipedia sources]]

{{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia07]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia07]] [[Category:Articles tagged as templates and modules]]

Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] doing this to split off the two wikipedia derived categories using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]]

{{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]]

A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]]

{{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Reftags]][[category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[category:kk]] [[category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Reftags]][[category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Reftags]][[category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Reftags]][[category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Reftags]][[category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]] [[category:1 revision imported]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[category:Help:Reftags]][[category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]]

There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq-Help-qq]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq-Help-qq]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq-Help-qq]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq-Help-qq]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq-Help-qq]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Help:Reftags]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq-Help-qq]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] {{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq-Help-qq]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] {{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[Category:kk]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[sn:Reftags]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[sn:Reftags]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[sn:Reftags]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[sn:Reftags]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[sn:Reftags]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Help:Reftags]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Reftags]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Wikipedia how to|H:RTAG|WP:RTAG}} This page, '''Help:Reftags''', explains the use of the reftag element,'''{{xtag|ref|p}}''' for defining reference [[wp:footnotes|footnotes]], as displayed by using a {{xtag|references|s}} tag or a {{tlx|Reflist}} template to list the footnotes. A reftag can also be defined by using wikitext function {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}} to generate the text {{nobr|...}} and allow direct use of [[wp:subst|subst]]'ing of templates to embed equivalent markup inside a footnote, or to nest a {{xtag|ref|p}} inside a {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...}}}}. == Defining a reftag element == The general format is text for a simple reftag footnote. However reftags can have a name="xx" to define a footnote to be reused on a page by named ref, {{nobr|}}, as when citing the same webpage at several spots in the article text. There can be more than 3,000 reftag footnotes defined on a page, but usually there are less than a hundred.A reftag formats extremely fast, but users can become overwhelmed with more than 500 footnotes on a page. ===Citing different pages in a single source=== {{See also|Wikipedia:Citing sources#Citing multiple pages of the same source|Help:References and page numbers#Named references}} When citing different pages in a single source, a named refbook details here can be followed by a {{tlx|R}} template. Given a ref like "book", the ref '''"author1990"''' can be reused, now using a different page number, say 53. This subsequent ref {{R|author1990|p=53}} will show the same superscript number, with a suffix of a colon and, in this case 53, the page number.{{R|author1990|p=53}} This format allows the reader to click to the reference section, and then click on other references from the same source. ===Named groupings=== {{See also|Help:Footnotes#Footnotes: groups}} Rather than define hundreds of footnotes in a single list, various reftags could split to some named groupings, such as using "..."" to collect those footnotes into group name "gg" as displayed by .This is a sample footnote in group="fn". For example, a common tactic is to define footnote group "fn" which shows each link as {{nobr|"[fn 9]"}} for the 9th footnote in the group="fn". A group name can be multiple words in straight double quotation marks (group={{nobr|"set xx yy"}}), but a single-word name with no punctuation or other special characters, just ASCII letters and numerals, can omit the quotation marks (as: group=fn). Hence, many group names are typically one-word labels, to avoid excessive quotation marks.This footnote uses double-quotes in name group="fn". == Defining a reftag function == Rather than using an element text, an alternate method is to use the wikitext function tag:ref, as {{nobr|{{#tag:ref|...text...}}}} to define the contents of a footnote. Examples: * Using {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Example of tag:ref function}} and list footnote "Example of tag:ref function" under the References. * Using named {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} will show a superscript number: {{#tag:ref|Named reftag function xx1|name="xx1"}} and list footnote "Named reftag function xx1" under the References below. The footnote name "xx1" then can be reused by putting {{nobr|}} at various points in the text. ==Footnotes (fn)== ==References== {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Advanced footnote formatting]] – special layouts * [[Wikipedia:Citing sources]] ([[WP:CITE]]) – how to format footnotes or bibliography * [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources]] ([[WP:RS]]) – about reputable references * [[Wikipedia:Find sources]] – how to hunt for quality source documents * {{tl|Cite web}} – most common citation [[Wikipedia:Template|template]], for sources with a URL * {{tl|Cite book}} – to cite a book/chapter or volume as a source reference * {{tl|Cite journal}} – to cite an article in a periodical as a source reference * [[mw:Help:Cite]] – original documentation of the {{xtag|ref}} citation feature provided by [[MediaWiki]] {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Reftags]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Reftags]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:Wikipedia sources]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Reftags]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Reftags]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Reftags]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Reftags]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[en:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Reftags]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Reftags]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Reftags]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Reftags]] [[Category:11-Help-11]] [[en:Reftags]][[en:Help:Reftags]][[Category:Help:Reftags]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Reftags]]
